speaker: doctor
text: Good morning. Let's start with your vitals — your heart rate is 70 bpm, which is within normal range.

speaker: patient
text: Thanks, Doctor. I’ve been feeling a bit fatigued lately, and my skin has been unusually dry.

speaker: doctor
text: I’ve prescribed Lisinopril 20mg once daily to help manage your blood pressure — we’ll monitor how you respond over the next few weeks.

speaker: patient
text: Okay, I’ll take it as directed. Should I watch for any side effects?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes — dizziness, persistent cough, or swelling in your face or lips warrant an immediate call. Also, avoid potassium supplements unless instructed.

speaker: doctor
text: Your temperature is elevated at 39.9 °C — that’s a significant fever. Have you had chills, sore throat, or recent exposure to illness?

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I’ve had chills since yesterday evening and a mild sore throat — no cough yet.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your symptoms and lab trends, we’re diagnosing hypothyroidism — likely contributing to your fatigue and cold intolerance. We’ll confirm with TSH and free T4 testing.

speaker: doctor
text: Your fasting blood glucose is 7.0 mmol/L — slightly above target for non-diabetics, so we’ll keep an eye on metabolic health alongside thyroid management.

speaker: doctor
text: Your systolic blood pressure is 105 mmHg today — lower than usual, possibly related to the fever or Lisinopril initiation. We’ll reassess after the acute illness resolves.

speaker: patient
text: That makes sense. I did feel lightheaded when standing up this morning.

speaker: doctor
text: Let’s hold the Lisinopril for now until your fever breaks and hydration improves — we don’t want further BP drops during acute illness.

speaker: patient
text: Understood. Should I check my blood sugar again before my next visit?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes — especially if you notice increased thirst or urination. We’ll also repeat it after starting levothyroxine, since thyroid status affects glucose metabolism.

speaker: patient
text: I started taking levothyroxine 50 mcg this morning, as you suggested last week.

speaker: doctor
text: Good — start with that dose and increase to 75 mcg in four weeks, assuming no palpitations or insomnia. Avoid taking it with calcium or iron supplements.

speaker: patient
text: Noted. I’ve been spacing it out from my multivitamin.

speaker: doctor
text: Excellent. Let’s also schedule a follow-up TSH in six weeks and repeat your glucose and renal panel before adjusting Lisinopril.

speaker: patient
text: Sounds good. I’ll bring my home glucose log next time.

speaker: patient
text: By the way, that reading from earlier — the blood glucose was 7.0 mmol/L — matches what I saw this morning too.
